aboutMeSection.title: I'm a
aboutMeSection.description: with 6 years of experience focused on back-end development using Java (Spring), Node.js, and Python to build web applications, microservices, and system integrations. On the front-end, I also have hands-on experience with React, Vue.js, Angular, HTML, and CSS. During my career, I have worked across new projects, production support, and continuous improvements, building solid experience in:| * Software architecture: monolithic and distributed systems (microservices), architecture design, component definition, cloud services (AWS), and communication between systems.| * High-performance and scalable software development: building robust APIs with clear documentation and automated tests, delivering safe and performant solutions with idempotent implementations, concurrency and transaction control, and good design practices.| * Databases: modeling, queries, and operations in relational databases (Oracle, PostgreSQL, MySQL) and non-relational databases (Redis, DynamoDB).| * Collaboration with multidisciplinary teams: working in agile environments such as Scrum and actively helping solve critical issues in financial systems to keep operations running smoothly.| * Monitoring and logging: applying observability best practices to improve visibility and speed up incident detection and resolution.

backEndStack.title: Back-end Development

cloudAndTools.title: Cloud & Tools
cloudAndTools.tools: AWS | Docker | JIRA / Monday | DataDog | Cursor AI | Github Copilot

warren.activities: Developed web applications and microservices using Java, Python, Node.js, and Vue.js.|Automated internal processes with VBA, optimizing data workflows in Excel.|Participated in the full software development lifecycle, from architectural design to deployment (Docker, AWS) and monitoring (DataDog, OpenTelemetry).|Refactored legacy systems, enhancing visibility, performance, and removing redundant code.|Optimized internal processes, improving operational efficiency by up to 80%.| Integrated systems with institutions like B3 and BACEN, reducing operational errors and associated costs.|Actively supported critical incidents, collaborating with internal teams and clients.

santander.activities: Worked on integrations between internal and third-party systems to automate processes, cutting processing time by up to 60% and reducing operational errors.|Modeled and developed procedures in relational databases (Oracle, PostgreSQL).|Joined architecture discussions, component definition, and API contract design before implementation started.|Improved and fixed issues in legacy systems.

btg.activities: Working on microservices projects to deliver new features, optimize processes, and automate manual tasks widely used by both operations teams and external clients.|Developing improvements and bug fixes in existing systems.|Designing and evolving relational database models (Oracle, PostgreSQL).|Participating in architecture discussions, component definition, and API contract design.|Actively using AWS services such as S3, SQS, SNS, and EC2.

projectsSection.title: Projects
projectsSection.inotecSummary: Temperature monitoring platform with connected sensors and a management dashboard.
projectsSection.inotecSpecs: ESP32 devices reading temperature and publishing via MQTT to AWS IoT Core.|Amazon SQS queue receiving readings for decoupled processing.|PostgreSQL storing sensor telemetry and historical data.|Web page to manage sensors and inspect each sensor's readings.
projectsSection.efCapitalSummary: Data intelligence platform for Brazilian companies, focused on filtering and enrichment.
projectsSection.efCapitalSpecs: Spring Batch pipeline processing files with millions of records.|Amazon SQS distributing lines/messages for parallel company processing.|PostgreSQL persistence layer for normalized and searchable company data.|Filtering workflow based on company characteristics plus manual enrichment.|Amazon Cognito used to isolate and secure data by user/account.
